growth_records.medium: Rich medium (mGAM / WCA / Schaedler) with and without supplemental mucin (Sigma M1778, type III)

up.method: observed better growth in presence of mucin

growth_records.cite: Ducarmon et al. Cayman enables large-scale analysis of gut microbiome carbohydrate-active enzyme repertoires. Nat Microbiol (2026). DOI:10.1038/s41564-026-02318-2. [PMC13236585]
growth_records.snippet: To experimentally test whether H. hathewayi, E. tayi and Coprobacter secundus are able to utilize mucin, we grew these species in media with and without mucin ... We observed better growth (faster outgrowth, increased growth rates and/or hi
growth_records.notes: OD578 growth curves only (Extended Data Fig.3); no numeric growth rate/doubling time/flux reported. Better growth with mucin (qualitative). Media are commercial undefined broths; composition not stated. One consolidated record for C. secundus mucin-utilization experiment.

phenotypes.base_medium: Sole-substrate growth / assimilation on a defined minimal medium (substrate as C/N/energy source) — positive = the organism grows on it
phenotypes.citation: BacDive strain 140126 (Reimer LC et al., BacDive 2022, NAR); https://bacdive.dsmz.de/strain/140126

phenotypes.base_medium: Enzyme / activity assay (hydrolysis, degradation, reduction, electron transfer) in a test medium — not a sole-substrate growth test

phenotypes.base_medium: Acid/gas-production test: peptone/complex base + substrate + pH indicator — positive = fermentative acid production, NOT growth on the substrate as a sole carbon source (interpret with care for model validation)
